reasoning: The kernel has an arithmetic intensity of ~0.00 FLOP/byte, far below the ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte, and DRAM bandwidth utilization is 60.4% of peak — well above the 50% threshold indicating memory bandwidth is the limiting resource. The kernel performs only a single comparison and selection per three memory accesses (two loads, one store), making it almost purely memory-bound. While long scoreboard stalls are high at 62.7%, this is consistent with warps waiting on global memory loads in a memory-bandwidth-saturated scenario rather than a latency-bound one, since DRAM utilization is substantial and occupancy is excellent at 92.8%.

The kernel performs only a single comparison and selection per three memory accesses (two loads, one store), making it almost purely memory-bound. While long scoreboard stalls are high at 62.7%, this is consistent with warps waiting on global memory loads in a memory-bandwidth-saturated scenario rather than a latency-bound one, since DRAM utilization is substantial and occupancy is excellent at 92.8%.

prompt.system: You are a GPU performance engineer. Follow the requested format exactly.

judge.explanation: The response correctly identifies memory-bound as the bottleneck with HIGH confidence, cites specific metric values (0.00 FLOP/byte arithmetic intensity, 60.4% DRAM BW utilization, 92.8% occupancy), and correctly demotes the misleading long scoreboard stall signal (62.7%) as a secondary symptom of memory latency rather than the primary cause.
